name: Papa Luna Route
name_original: Peña Guzmán y Alto de Cabrera — circular por la Sierra de la Virgen desde Illueca
description: Hike the difficult Papa Luna Route in Sierra de la Virgen, a 8.8-mile (14.1 km) trail with 2746 feet (837 metres) of elevation gain.
region_intro: Hiking around Illueca features a landscape characterized by varied terrain, including significant hills and parts of the Sierra de la Virgen mountain range. The region offers routes with notable elevation changes, leading to panoramic views. Hikers can expect to traverse paths that include both challenging ascents and more moderate sections. Best hiking trails around Illueca The most popular hiking route is Papa Luna Route, a 8.8 miles (14.1 km) trail that takes 5 hours 3 minutes to complete. This challenging route features substantial elevation gain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pico Cabrera from Sestrica — circular via the Sierra de la Virgen, a difficult 12.0 miles (19.2 km) path. This trail offers expansive views across the Sierra de la Virgen. Local hikers also love the Peña del Café – Peña de Guzmán loop from Illueca, a 12.7 miles (20.5 km) trail leading through hilly terrain with notable peaks, often completed in about 6 hours 53 minutes. Hiking around Illueca is defined by its hills, mountain ranges like the Sierra de la Virgen, and varied elevation profiles. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging ascents. The routes in Illueca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore Illueca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking around Illueca?
A: Hiking around Illueca features a landscape characterized by varied terrain, including significant hills and parts of the Sierra de la Virgen mountain range. You can expect paths with notable elevation changes, offering both challenging ascents and more moderate sections.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails in Illueca?
A: The hiking trails directly around Illueca are generally considered moderate to difficult, with no easy routes listed in our current selection. However, the region offers a variety of paths, and some sections of the moderate trails may be suitable for those looking for less strenuous options.

Q: How long do hiking trails in Illueca typically take to complete?
A: The duration of hikes in Illueca varies depending on the route and your pace. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e972119894/papa-luna-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Papa Luna Route&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 14.1 km trail that typically takes around 5 hours 3 minutes. Longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Café – Peña de Guzmán loop from Illueca&lt;/a&gt;, at 20.5 km, can take closer to 7 hours.

Q: Are there any circular walks available in Illueca?
A: Yes, Illueca offers several circular walks. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979996918/pico-cabrera-from-sestrica-circular-via-the-sierra-de-la-virgen" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Cabrera from Sestrica — circular via the Sierra de la Virgen&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 19.2 km path offering expansive views. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Café – Peña de Guzmán loop from Illueca&lt;/a&gt;, which is 20.5 km long.

Q: What are some challenging hikes in the Illueca area?
A: Illueca is known for its challenging hikes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e972119894/papa-luna-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Papa Luna Route&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 14.1 km trail with substantial elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979996918/pico-cabrera-from-sestrica-circular-via-the-sierra-de-la-virgen" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Cabrera from Sestrica — circular via the Sierra de la Virgen&lt;/a&gt; is another difficult option, spanning 19.2 km with significant ascents.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I see along the hiking trails?
A: Hikers can discover several interesting landmarks and natural features. You might encounter the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337753" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle-Palace of Pope Luna&lt;/a&gt; or the natural monument of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2920672" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Gollizno&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features prominent peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5286167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Cabrera&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5286155" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de Guzmán&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning views.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Illueca?
A: The hiking routes in Illueca are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the challenging ascents leading to panoramic views, and the opportunity to explore the Sierra de la Virgen mountain range.

Q: Are there any hikes that pass by the Castle-Palace of Pope Luna?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38160977" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle-Palace of Pope Luna loop from Brea de Aragón&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 15.5 km hike that specifically features this historic landmark. It's a great way to combine hiking with cultural exploration.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Illueca?
A: Given the significant elevation changes and potential for challenging conditions, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for hiking in Illueca. Temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be hot, and winter may bring colder temperatures and snow at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any specific peaks or viewpoints worth seeking out?
A: Absolutely. The region is home to several notable peaks and viewpoints. &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5286167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Cabrera (1,433 m)&lt;/a&gt; in the Sierra de la Virgen offers expansive views. Other prominent summits include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5286155" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de Guzmán&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5286163" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Café&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5834920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Castillo de Mesones de Isuela and Moncayo&lt;/a&gt; from the descent to Nigüella.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in the Illueca area?
A: There are over 7 hiking routes available around Illueca, ranging from moderate to difficult in intensity. This selection provides options for various fitness levels, all offering a chance to explore the region's hilly and mountainous terrain.
tips: Located at an altitude of 1,352 meters, we are in Peña de Guzmán with magnificent views between Mingoaranda and Peña del Café, all located in the Sierra de la Virgen.
At 1,412 meters above sea level is the rocky peak of Peña del Café. 1 km away is what separates us from Peña Guzmán, both located in the Sierra de la Virgen.
Located at an altitude of 1,433 meters, we find the cairn at the top of Alto de Cabrera, with a panoramic view of the Sierra de la Virgen.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Papa Luna Route?
faqs.answer: The Papa Luna Route is considered a difficult hike. It involves significant elevation gain and requires a good level of physical fitness and preparation. Hikers should be ready for a challenging experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Papa Luna Route?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the Papa Luna Route trailhead is not detailed, but as the route starts from Illueca and explores the Sierra de la Virgen, it's advisable to look for designated parking areas within or near Illueca that provide access to the Sierra de la Virgen trails. Always check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is the Papa Luna Route suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on the Papa Luna Route are not provided, trails in natural parks like the Sierra de la Virgen often permit dogs on a leash. It's always best to keep your dog under control, especially given the challenging terrain and potential for wildlife encounters. Ensure you carry enough water for your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike the Papa Luna Route, especially given its challenging nature and location in the Sierra de la Virgen, is typically during spring or autumn. In spring, you might encounter slopes blanketed with alpine flowers. These seasons offer more moderate temperatures, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential snow or ice in winter at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Sierra de la Virgen?
faqs.answer: There are generally no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Sierra de la Virgen for hiking. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning environmental protection and any specific rules that might apply to certain areas within the natural park.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Papa Luna Route?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this route is rugged and challenging. You can expect paths that run along mountain ridges, with sections that may be shaded by ancient pines. Given the significant elevation changes, prepare for varied surfaces that demand sure-footedness and appropriate hiking footwear.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Papa Luna Route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable points of interest, including prominent summits. You'll pass by Peña de Guzmán, Peña del Café, and the highest point, Pico Cabrera (1,433 m) – Sierra de la Virgen. These offer sweeping panoramas of the surrounding landscape.

faqs.question: Is the Papa Luna Route a circular trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Papa Luna Route is a circular hike. This means you will end your journey at or near your starting point, offering a complete and immersive experience without needing to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a hike on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and duration, pack essentials like plenty of water, high-energy snacks, appropriate hiking boots, layers of clothing for changing weather, sun protection, a map or GPS device, and a first-aid kit. Trekking poles can also be beneficial for the elevation changes.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the way?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The route is known for its sweeping panoramas, especially from the summits like Peña de Guzmán and Pico Cabrera (1,433 m) – Sierra de la Virgen. You'll find consistent views of the mountain ridge and the diverse landscape of the Sierra de la Virgen.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the Papa Luna Route?
faqs.answer: As the route starts from Illueca, you will find amenities like cafes and restaurants in the town itself. However, there are no facilities directly on the trail, so ensure you carry all necessary provisions for the duration of your hike.

waypoints.name: Pico Cabrera (1,433 m) – Sierra de la Virgen
